task_completion.description: The main body of the brick is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A square block is visible in the FreeCAD 3D view, or a corresponding Pad/Box feature is present in the tree view.

task_completion.description: Four studs are created on the top of the brick.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four cylindrical protrusions arranged in a 2x2 grid are visible on the top face of the brick in the 3D view.

task_completion.description: The interior of the brick is hollowed out from the bottom.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view shows the bottom of the brick is open/hollow, or a 'Thickness', 'Pocket', or 'Shell' feature is visible in the tree view.

task_completion.description: A central reinforcement tube is created inside the hollowed interior.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A hollow cylindrical tube is visible inside the bottom cavity of the brick, or corresponding features (like a Pad with a hole or a Tube) are visible in the tree view.

task_completion.description: The final model is combined into a single solid body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tree view shows a single active Body or a final Boolean 'Fusion'/'Cut' operation combining all parts, rather than multiple separate independent bodies.

task_completion.description: The file is saved as brick_2x2.FCStd.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title or the root item in the tree view displays 'brick_2x2', or a file manager shows the file saved in /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeCAD interface with toolbars, tree view, and 3D view being actively used, rather than just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The model was built step-by-step rather than imported.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tree view shows a history of operations (e.g., Sketches, Pads, Pockets, Thickness) rather than a single imported mesh or step file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modeling a 2x2 LEGO-compatible brick in FreeCAD. Dimensions: body 15.8x15.8x9.6 mm, 4 top studs (4.8 mm diameter, 1.8 mm height, 8 mm spacing), hollow interior with 1.2 mm wall thickness, and a central reinforcement tube (6.5 mm OD, 4.8 mm ID). The final file must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/brick_2x2.FCStd.
